rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing Victor gaskets attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Pearson precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Union Carbide¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6 (sophisticated intermediary defense, bulk supplier defense and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages, there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his cause of action for false representation, although the court sees no dismissal on file. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE SEEVERS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Defendant Union Carbide¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6-8 (sophisticated intermediary defense, bulk supplier defenses, loss of consortium and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages (issue 7), plaintiffs are not seeking punitive damages based on conduct that occurred in another state. Rather, plaintiffs seek punitive damages based on defendant¿s mining, supply, sale and distribution of raw asbestos fiber, all of which occurred within California. As to punitive damages (issue 8), there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their cause of action for false representation, although the court sees no dismissal on file. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. Carrier Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication is continued to March 2 at the request of plaintiff pursuant to CCP 437c(h). Supplemental opposition papers, if any, must be filed and served by February 16. Supplemental reply papers, if any, must be filed and served by February 23. Plaintiff¿s papers show that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot now be presented. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. Defendant Cooper Industries, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. By not presenting contention interrogatory responses from plaintiff with its moving papers, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for punitive damages, which was the sole basis for the alternate motion for summary adjudication. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. Defendant Tingue, Brown & Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing pads attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST STONE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5. Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he purchased asbestos-containing auto parts from defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement fails to show that any of the auto parts that plaintiff purchased from defendant did not contain asbestos other than a) brakes purchased in 1985 or later, b) Bendix, Raybestos and Borg-Warner clutch parts and automotive gaskets, and c) Victor and Fel-Pro automotive gaskets from 1950-92..Plaintiff represents that he dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages, the sole grounds for the alternate motion for summary adjudication, although the court sees no dismissal on Lexis. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DUNNIGAN et al VS. CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. Defendant Goodman Lumber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed January 20. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DUNNIGAN et al VS. CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Dunnigan was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant¿s employees. Mr. Dunnigan¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement On Terms That Comply With The Medicare Secondary Payer Act, 42 Usc Section 1395(B)(2)
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 8. Defendant Ford Motor Co.¿s motion to enforce settlement on terms that comply with the Medicare Secondary Payer Act is continued to February 2 on the court¿s own motion to provide the parties with additional time to resolve the issues raised by the motion. If the parties are not able to resolve the issues raised by the motion, the motion will not be heard by a judge other than Judge Kahn. Judge Kahn will recuse himself from deciding this motion due to his participation in ex parte settlement discussions with the parties regarding Medicare release language. = (220/HEK)
